package main

import (
	"log"
)

// Positive scenario for domain_identifiers_logged_under_inconsistent_field_keys.
// Rule intent: The same entity ID being logged under many different keys across
// packages, making log aggregation and querying unreliable.

type UserService struct{}

func (s *UserService) CreateUser(userID string) {
	// Logs user_id with snake_case
	log.Printf("user_id=%s action=create", userID)
}

func (s *UserService) GetUser(userID string) {
	// Logs the same identifier as userId with camelCase
	log.Printf("userId=%s action=get", userID)
}

func (s *UserService) DeleteUser(userID string) {
	// Logs the same identifier as user-id with kebab-case
	log.Printf("user-id=%s action=delete", userID)
}

type OrderService struct{}

func (s *OrderService) CreateOrder(orderID string, userID string) {
	// Logs order_id but user as uid
	log.Printf("order_id=%s uid=%s action=create_order", orderID, userID)
}

func (s *OrderService) GetOrder(orderID string) {
	// Logs order_id as orderId (inconsistent with CreateOrder)
	log.Printf("orderId=%s action=get_order", orderID)
}

type AuditService struct{}

func (s *AuditService) LogEvent(entityID string) {
	// Logs the same entity identifier as entity, id, and ref across calls
	log.Printf("entity=%s event=audit", entityID)
	log.Printf("id=%s event=audit", entityID)
	log.Printf("ref=%s event=audit", entityID)
}
